utility_evaluation.REASONING: First, count the number of items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget — total of 5 items. Next, check the final coverage plan to see if these items are present. All 5 items are present: health_coverage is specified with plan, cost, and benefits (covers physical and mental health with costs). Property_coverage is specified with plan, cost, and benefits (home/contents with costs). Travel_coverage is specified with plan, cost, and benefits (travel insurance with costs). Additional_services is present with a list of enhancement services, but no explicit costs are mentioned — the format requires 'with costs', so this is incomplete. Total_budget has monthly and annual costs, which is complete. Therefore, 4 out of 5 items are fully specified with all required details.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists three main insurance plans: PrimeCare Premium Plan under health_coverage, EliteHome Premium Coverage under property_coverage, and PrimeTravel Premium Plan under travel_coverage. To map these to ratings, I need to find each plan in the ground-truth ratings. For PrimeCare Premium Plan, it appears under primary_health_coverage -> physical_health with rating 10 (and also under mental_health with rating 10, but since the plan name is the same, the rating is consistently 10). EliteHome Premium Coverage is found under property_protection -> home_content with a rating of 10. PrimeTravel Premium Plan is found under mobility_coverage -> travel_insurance with a rating of 10. Therefore, all three selected plans have a ground-truth rating of 10.
